records.ety: [L. scriptura, fr. scribere, scriptum, to write: cf. OF. escripture, escriture, F. criture. See Scribe.]

defs: Anything written; a writing; a document; an inscription.
quotes: I have put it in scripture and in remembrance.
Then the Lord of Manny read the scripture on the tomb, the which was in Latin.

defs: The books of the Old and the New Testament, or of either of them; the Bible; -- used by way of eminence or distinction, and chiefly in the plural.
quotes: There is not any action a man ought to do, or to forbear, but the Scripture will give him a clear precept or prohibition for it.
Compared with the knowledge which the Scriptures contain, every other subject of human inquiry is vanity.

defs: A passage from the Bible; a text.
quotes: The devil can cite Scripture for his purpose.
Hanging by the twined thread of one doubtful Scripture.
